rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL SHEPHERD VS. BLOOM ENERGY CORPORATION
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Petition By Michael Shepherd To Confirm Final Arbitration Decision
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for Mar-19-2025, Line 1. 1-PETITIONER MICHAEL SHEPHERD'S Petition To Confirm Final Arbitration Decision. The complete tentative ruling has been sent to counsel via email. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: HORACIO CAMPOS VS. KEZAR PUB INC. ET AL

rulings.Case Title: CREDIT CORP SOLUTIONS INC., VS. JASON JONES ET AL
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for Mar-19-2025, Line 3. CROSS DEFENDANT CREDIT CORP SOLUTIONS INC.'S, ASSIGNEE OF SYNCHRONY BANK, DEMURRER to Amended CROSS COMPLAINT. The hearing is continued to March 28, 2025. The Aguirre declaration shows that Cross-Complainant Jason Jones's counsel did not meet and confer notwithstanding Cross-Defendant Credit Corp. Solutions's efforts. The parties are ordered to meet and confer telephonically or in person as required by CCP 430.41 prior to the continued hearing date. If meet and confer efforts result in agreement about the Cross-Complaint the parties may submit a stipulated proposed order prior to the continued date. Both parties are further ordered to provide courtesy copies of their papers consistent with Local Rule 2.7(B) for the continued hearing date, with a cover letter showing the new hearing date. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: STATE FARM MUTUAL AUTOMOBILE INSURANCE VS. JAVIER BARAJAS PEREZ ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Deem Requests For Admissions Admitted And Of Nonappearance
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for Mar-19-2025, Line 5. 1-PLAINTIFF STATE FARM MUTUAL AUTOMOBILE INSURANCE'S Motion To Deem Requests For Admissions Admitted And Of Nonappearance. Plaintiff State Farm Mutual Automobile Insurance's motion to deem admitted the truth of any matters specified in its Requests for Admission, Set One, served on defendant Pacific Structures, Inc., is granted. Defendant failed to timely respond to this discovery and has thereby waived objections. (CCP 2033.280(a).) The truth of any matters specified in the RFAs, Set One, is deemed admitted by defendant Pacific Structures Inc. (CCP 2033.280(b), (c).) Plaintiff also seeks sanctions. CCP 2033.280(c) provides: "It is mandatory that the court impose a monetary sanction under Chapter 7 (commencing with Section 2023.010) on the party or attorney, or both, whose failure to serve a timely response to requests for admission necessitated this motion." The court awards sanctions of $460 payable by defendant to plaintiff. Defendant shall remit sanctions within 10 court days of entry of this order. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)
